sections.paragraph:
(yגkפsh) 1 pers. יָקשְׁתּי i.q. נָקַשׁ and קֹושׁ (which see), to lay snares, to be a birdcatcher. Part. יֹוקֵשׁ a fowler, 124:7 . Followed by לְ of pers., Jer 50:24 and more fully יָקשׁ פַּח לְ to lay snares for, i.e. to plot against any one. (Fut. יְקשׁוּן Isa 29:21, is from קֹושׁ.)

Niphal נֹוקֵשׁ, to be snared, to be taken in a noose, Isa 8:15 28:13 Pro 6:2. Metaphorically to be snared, or seduced by avarice, Deu 7:25.

Pual, part. יוּקָשִׁים for מְיוּקָּשִׁים Ecc 9:12.

Derivatives, יָקֹושׁ, מֹוקֵשׁ, and-
